$MELI issued nearly 3 million new credit cards in a single quarter. Some investors see this rapid credit expansion and worry about risk. There is a bigger picture. This is not short-term noise. It is about locking in LTV

First, let's look at the raw numbers from Q4 2025. The total credit portfolio nearly doubled YoY to $12.5 billion. They successfully pushed out 3 million new credit cards right during the peak holiday shopping season.

Why issue so many cards? It is not just about collecting interest or late fees. The real goal is marketplace stickiness. The credit segment is the ultimate funnel to keep users inside the MELI ecosystem.

When a shopper gets a Mercado Pago card, their behavior changes entirely.
Management confirmed this in their latest earnings call. Once a user adopts the credit card, their overall engagement goes up. Their total net spend on the ecom platform rises significantly.

The margins are also much better. When a user pays with a 3P card, there is friction. When they pay with a Mercado Pago card on MercadoLibre, the company wins twice. Management actually calls this "double dipping."

MELI controls the checkout platform. MELI also controls the credit facility. Because they own both sides of the transaction, processing costs drop. At the same time, transaction approval rates skyrocket. This structurally raises the profit per customer.

The final piece of the puzzle is retention. Mercado Pago just hit a historic milestone. They now hold the number one Net Promoter Score in Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, and Chile.

Internal data shows a direct link: when users adopt credit products, their NPS and retention rates climb. They get access to extra installments and better cashback. They have no reason to shop anywhere else.

MercadoLibre's credit growth is not just an isolated, risky revenue stream. It is a calculated ecosystem lock-in. They are using credit to acquire high-LTV customers who will buy more, cost less to serve, and never leave. The incredible growth in credit cards is a strong leading indicator for their future imo.
